specification.topic-statement: The document cache stores the contents of files referenced by internal URIs in memory, eliminating repeated filesystem reads when the same document is requested multiple times.

scope.in-scope: Cache entry structure, on-demand loading from the filesystem, URI-to-path translation before lookup, cache miss handling, single-entry invalidation, bulk clear, and error handling when a file cannot be loaded.
scope.boundary: How internal URIs are resolved to canonical filesystem paths is handled by the paths subsystem and is out of scope here.
How cached document contents are concatenated to form the system prompt is handled by the LLM request assembly subsystem and is out of scope here.

field.name: canonical path
field.note: absolute filesystem path after URI translation; used as the lookup key

field.name: content
field.note: raw file bytes as a null-terminated string; owned by the cache

field.name: entries
field.note: starts with capacity 4; doubles on overflow

behavior.lookup:
A document is retrieved by supplying its internal URI. The URI is first translated to a canonical filesystem path. If translation fails, an error is returned immediately and the cache is not consulted.
The cache entries are scanned linearly for an entry whose canonical path matches. On a hit, the stored content pointer is returned. The pointer is owned by the cache and must not be freed by the caller; it remains valid until the entry is invalidated or the cache is cleared.
behavior.cache-miss-loading:
On a cache miss, the file is read from the filesystem at the canonical path. If the read succeeds, a new entry is added to the cache with the canonical path and the file content, and the content is returned. If the array is at capacity, it is doubled before the entry is added.
If the file cannot be read (for example, because it does not exist or is not accessible), an error is returned and no entry is added to the cache.
The file content is allocated within the cache's memory context. Callers receive a borrowed pointer and must not attempt to free it.
behavior.invalidation:
A single cache entry can be invalidated by supplying its internal URI. The URI is translated to a canonical path, and the matching entry (if any) is removed. Remaining entries are shifted to fill the gap.
If URI translation fails or no matching entry is found, the operation returns without modifying the cache.
Bulk invalidation (clear) frees all entries, resets the count to zero, and leaves the capacity and array allocation intact for reuse.
behavior.use-by-pinned-documents:
The primary consumer is the system prompt assembly process. When building an effective system prompt from pinned documents, each pinned document URI is looked up in the cache in order. If a document fails to load, it is silently skipped and the next pinned document is processed.
Bulk cache clears occur when the tool registry is refreshed (via the `/refresh` command), ensuring that document contents are re-read from disk if files have changed since they were last cached.
behavior.per-agent-isolation: Each agent owns a separate document cache instance. There is no shared cache across agents. An agent's cache is freed when the agent is destroyed.
behavior.allocation-failure: Memory allocation failures during cache growth or content storage cause an immediate process abort.
